rulings.Case Title: CLAYTON HARMSTON et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, and, HEATHER FONG MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Is continued to July 22, 2010 on the Court's own motion. Plaintiff to file and serve a new opposition that complies with the California Rules of Court regarding page limitations by 7/8/2010. Any new reply shall be filed and served by 7/15/2010. See California Rules of Court 3.1113(d). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: J K SELZNICK VS. ANDREW M ZACKS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 2, PLAINTIFF J SELZNICK Motion To Strike Costs. Off Calendar, as moot in veiw of filing of motion for post-judgment collection attorneys fees. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: J K SELZNICK VS. ANDREW M ZACKS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Post-Judgment Collection Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANTS ANDREW ZACKS, ZACKS, UTRECHT & LEADBETTER, P.C. Motion for Post-Judgment Collection Attorneys Fees. Motion granted in part. Requested amount reduced by $576 for duplicate entry and errors. Defendant awarded reasonable attorney fees in the amount of $13,642. See Gilbert vs. Master Washer & Stamping Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App. 4th 212. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MATT VASQUEZ et al VS. DAN RUSH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT DANIEL RUSH DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead more facts regarding the terms of the contract, specifics regarding the fraud, and basis for restitution. See Dunkin vs. Boskey (2000) 82 Cal.App. 4th 171, 196. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARIANNE BECK et al VS. PRANA EIGHT PROPERTIES LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Supplemental Atty'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT PRANA EIGHT PROPERTIES, LLC Motion for Supplemental Attorneys Fees. Is granted. Defendant is awarded $3,078 additional attorney fees plus $40 costs. Requested attorney fees are reasonable and necessary. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SHANEL STASZ VS. ESTATE OF HUGO W. QUACKENBUSH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT ESTATE OF HUGO W. QUACKENBUSH MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Is granted. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate proper service in compliance with Probate Code section 552 and CCP section 416.90. There is no showing the Bragg is the person that the insurer designated in writing, or that there is an agency relationship between Bragg and the insurance company. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LIDIYA DEMICHEVA VS. DOMINIC TSE M.D et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT DOMINIC TSE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Is denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. The records on which the defendant's expert relied were not properly admitted under the business records exception to the hearsay rule. See Garibay vs. Hemmat (2008) 161 Cal.App. 4th 735. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections are sustained. Defendant's reply and declarations stricken as untimely. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP CHANT et al VS. FEDEX GROUND PACKAGE SYSTEM, INC, et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANTS FEDEX GROUND PACKAGE SYSTEM, INC, RAFAEL RODRIGUEZ MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is denied. Plaintiff's first amended complaint alleges that defendant requires its drivers to deliver an unrealistically high volume of packages per day and is aware that it induces drivers to "cut corners" with regard to safety. Plaintiffs' pleading is sufficient, and defendant raises a factual issue. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GREAT AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK, A et al VS. U.S. FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT U.S. FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is overruled, 10 days leave to answer factual issues not appropriately raised or resolved in a demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARIANNE BECK et al VS. PRANA EIGHT PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion An Dmotion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 9, PLAINTIFFS MARIANNE BECK, PHIL HEAD, KURT MUELLER, RYAN MCCAULEY, and, JOHN TYNAN, Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint. Is granted, in view of defendant's non-opposition to amendment, per se. Amended complaint will be filed and served. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARIANNE BECK et al VS. PRANA EIGHT PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Prana Eight Properties, Llc'S Motion To Strike Complaint (Continued From 06/29/10 To 06/30/10)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 9, Defendant Prana Eight Properties, LLC's Motion to Strike Complaint (Continued From 06/29/10 To 06/30/10). Is granted, no opposition filed. Defendant may bring a separate motion for attorney fees and costs. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANTS ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY, AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is continued to 9/28/2010 per amended notice. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK ROZENGURT et al VS. ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT SHARON BONE AMENDED DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is continued to 9/28/2010 per amended notice. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA MANGAN VS. KEY MORTGAGE CORP DBA KMC MORTGAGE CORP
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-10-632975 WITH CASE CGC-10-500318
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 11, PLAINTIFF ANDREA MANGAN MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-10-632975 WITH CASE CGC-10-500318. Is granted, if moving party provides proof of service and courtesy copies. Otherwise, Off Calendar. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE VS. ANN GARRISON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Continuance Of Pltf'S Motion For Summary Judgment Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 12, PLAINTIFF DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, Amended Notice of Continuance Of Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment Hearing. Is granted. Plaintiff presents evidence of validly conducted foreclosure sale, a proper 3-day notice and defendant's occupancy. Defendant fails to raise a triable issue of material fact to defeat plaintiff's right to possession. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY AS TRUSTEE VS. ERIKA VEGA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 12, DEFENDANT ERIKA VEGA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, amended complaint filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LARAMAR SF URBAN APARTMENTS VS. MATTHEW HENRY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 13, DEFENDANT MATTHEW HENRY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, dismissal of entire action filed on 6/8/2010. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSEE FOR VS. MARIE FLOR POWER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 16, PLAINTIFF U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Is denied, without prejudice. Defendant failed to shift the burden because defendant fails to show that it recorded a substitution of trustee. See CC section 2934a; Pro Value Properties, Inc. vs Quality Loan Service Corp. (2009) 170 Cal.App. 4th 579. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TERESA CASTELLO VS. SARA DOREMUS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 14, DEFENDANT SARA DOREMUS MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, Amended Complaint filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 30, 2010, line 15, DEFENDANT LUIS COELHO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, Amended Complaint filed. =(302/CWW)
